title: Argentina CUIT Registration / Data Modification for Natural Persons (Solicitud de Inscripción / Modificación de Datos — Personas Físicas y Sucesiones Indivisas, F.460/F)
description: AFIP's (Administración Federal de Ingresos Públicos) combined request form for natural persons (personas físicas) and undivided estates (sucesiones indivisas) to either newly register for a CUIT (Clave Única de Identificación Tributaria — Argentina's federal tax identifier) or to update previously-registered data, using a single-copy, 2-page fillable-AcroForm specimen, Formulario 460/F. This is the natural-person analogue of ar/afip/inscripcion-cuit-personas-juridicas (the legal-entity F.460/J), authored in a prior cycle — the two forms serve different applicant types via distinct specimens and are not variants of the same process. Unlike F.460/J, this specimen carries no mirrored 'Original'/'Duplicado' page pair (it is a single 2-page copy: 'Hoja 1 de 2' covers identification, nationality/residency, name, fiscal/real addresses, and phone/email; 'Hoja 2 de 2' covers economic activity, commercial data, tax/withholding-regime registrations, social-security data, and the signed sworn declaration) and no split-digit/split-component text-box groups (every field, including the CUIT itself, is a single open AcroForm text box — confirmed programmatically via maxLen across all 148 widgets, see VERIFICATION.md). This specimen also carries 9 genuine native PDF radio-button groups (confirmed via getFieldObjects()' radioButton:true and 2 widget instances each) and 8 combo boxes with closed AFIP-defined value catalogs (confirmed via each combo box's own 'items' list) — richer, programmatically-discoverable choice structures than the sibling specimen's, which had neither. Radio groups are modelled as type:enum per spec v0.3 §6.4.1; combo boxes with closed catalogs are also modelled as type:enum. This document deliberately does not model: the AFIP-staff-only reception handling of a filed paper form; the wet-ink 'Firma y Sello' (signature and seal), which carries no AcroForm widget; and the in-person/postal submission process itself (this specimen is a paper AcroForm meant to be printed, signed, and physically filed — there is no live online wizard for this specific form). GovSchema is independent and is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or operated by the Argentine Republic, the Ministerio de Economía, or AFIP.

source.documentRef: F. 460/F 'Solicitud de Inscripción / Modificación de Datos — Personas Físicas y Sucesiones Indivisas', Administración Federal de Ingresos Públicos (AFIP), 2-page fillable-AcroForm PDF

verification.notes: GOV-2195: closes out the F.460/F candidate named as a backlog item in GOV-2169's VERIFICATION.md (the personas-jurídicas F.460/J cycle), deepening Argentina's Business Formation vertical with its natural-person analogue. Fetched fresh with a browser User-Agent (HTTP 200, application/pdf, 208,143 bytes, genuine %PDF-1.6, SHA-256 be2465f8e8c52ef6cde52df6e8a0365c2a6575724a10c210d43396433829b699), independently re-fetched a second time this session (byte-for-byte and hash-for-hash identical) directly from serviciosweb.afip.gob.ar with no login/CAPTCHA/WAF gate encountered. Last-Modified: Fri, 12 Aug 2022 15:47:53 GMT. Extracted with pdfjs-dist (getFieldObjects()/getAnnotations({intent:'display'})/getTextContent(), x/y-position-based label attribution, independently re-run this session against getAnnotations() to confirm the raw widget count and check for any /Ff Required bit): 148 raw AcroForm widgets across 2 pages (48 p.1, 100 p.2, confirmed via page.getAnnotations()), 0 of 148 carrying the Required bit (fieldFlags & 2), across 7 distinct fieldFlags values none of which set that bit — checked programmatically, not eyeballed. getFieldObjects() resolves these to 180 distinct field names; 41 are non-terminal hierarchy container nodes (0 terminal widgets of their own, e.g. 'Apellido' → 'Apellido.0' → the terminal 'Apellido.0.1') and are excluded, leaving 139 real terminal logical fields — of which 130 have exactly 1 widget instance (independent text/combo/checkbox fields) and 9 have exactly 2 widget instances each, confirmed radioButton:true, genuine native PDF radio-button groups (Tramite, argentino o extranjero, residenteonoresidente, comp soc, comp soc 2, comp soc 3, comp soc 4, Tipo empleador, Telefonotipo) — unlike the personas-jurídicas sibling, which has no PDF radio-group layer at all. No maxLen constraint was found on any of the 139 fields (checked programmatically across every text-type entry in fieldObjects.json) — this specimen has no split-digit/split-component box groups to merge, unlike the sibling's CUIT1-11/dependencia1-3/pers1-5 groups; every field, including 'CUIT' itself, is already a single logical AcroForm field. Reconciliation: 148 raw widgets − 9 (one redundant widget per radio group, since each group's 2 widgets collapse into 1 logical enum field) = 139 logical fields, matching len(fields) after assembly exactly. 8 combo-box fields (motivo, tipodocumento, Mes cierre ej 2, caracter3, tiporesidencia, provincias.0, provincias.1, Caracter) carry closed AFIP-defined value catalogs, extracted directly from each combo box's own getFieldObjects() 'items' array (not guessed or typed from memory) and modelled as type:enum. See VERIFICATION.md for the full sourcing record, every judgment call (including a disclosed PDF-authoring quirk found via this document's own independent rect-based verification: the 'Tipo empleador' radio group's literal AcroForm exportValues are 'hecho'/'otras' — the same literal strings the unrelated 'Componente de sociedad' radio groups use — even though its own adjacent printed caption reads 'Común'/'Personal de casas particulares'), and the mock conformance test run.

fields.name: requestType
fields.label: Trámite
fields.description: Native PDF radio-button group (field name 'Tramite', getFieldObjects() confirms radioButton:true, 2 widget instances, exportValues 'Solic'/'modif') selecting whether this filing newly registers the individual for a CUIT ('Solic' = 'Solicitud de inscripción') or updates data for an already-registered individual ('modif' = 'Modificación de datos'). Modelled as type:enum per spec v0.3 §6.4.1, not a boolean pair, since this is a genuine single PDF radio-group layer (unlike the independent, non-grouped checkboxes used for analogous choices in the personas-jurídicas sibling). Mutual exclusivity is inherent to the enum type; required left false since the source PDF asserts no required-field signal anywhere (see VERIFICATION.md), though a filing without either value selected would not be practically valid.
fields.sourceRef: F.460/F p.1, form header — 'Motivo del trámite' radio: 'Solicitud de inscripción' / 'Modificación de datos'

fields.name: cuit
fields.label: CUIT
fields.description: The individual's 11-digit CUIT (Clave Única de Identificación Tributaria). A single open AcroForm text box (no maxLen, not split into per-digit boxes as in the personas-jurídicas sibling's 'cuit' field — confirmed no split-digit-box groups exist anywhere in this specimen, see VERIFICATION.md). Left blank on a first-time 'Solicitud de inscripción' (a CUIT does not yet exist for the individual being registered); expected to be populated on a 'Modificación de datos' filing (see crossFieldValidation).
fields.sourceRef: F.460/F p.1, form header — 'CUIT'

fields.name: requestReason
fields.label: Motivo del trámite
fields.description: AFIP-defined reason for this filing, selected from a closed AcroForm combo-box catalog (getFieldObjects() 'items', field name 'motivo'): spontaneous filing, a migratory-agreement ('Convenio migratorio') filing, or a filing made by official order ('Oficio'). Distinct from requestType (which states whether this is a new registration or a data modification) — this specimen positions the combo box directly beneath a 'Motivo del trámite' caption, separate from the 'Tramite' radio group's own inline option labels.
fields.sourceRef: F.460/F p.1, form header — 'Motivo del trámite' combo box

fields.name: documentType
fields.label: Tipo de documento
fields.description: Type of identity document, selected from a closed AcroForm combo-box catalog (field name 'tipodocumento').
fields.sourceRef: F.460/F p.1, 1- Datos de Identificación — 'Tipo de documento'

fields.name: documentNumber
fields.label: N° de documento
fields.description: Identity document number corresponding to documentType.
fields.sourceRef: F.460/F p.1, 1- Datos de Identificación — 'N° de documento'

fields.name: dateOfBirth
fields.label: Fecha de nacimiento
fields.description: Date of birth. Single open AcroForm text box (~53pt wide, matching this specimen's full DD/MM/AAAA-width convention observed across every other confirmed full-date field — see VERIFICATION.md); represented as an ISO 8601 full-date.
fields.sourceRef: F.460/F p.1, 1- Datos de Identificación — 'Fecha de nacimiento'

fields.name: sex
fields.label: Sexo (tal como figura en documento)
fields.description: Sex as it appears on the identity document named in documentType/documentNumber. Modelled as an open string rather than a closed enum: the AcroForm field ('Sexo') is a plain text box, not a combo box, unlike documentType/tipodocumento's own combo box just above it in the same row — the specimen does not constrain this to a fixed catalog the way it does for other choice fields.
fields.sourceRef: F.460/F p.1, 1- Datos de Identificación — 'Sexo (tal como figura en documento)'

fields.name: dateOfDeath
fields.label: Fecha de fallecimiento (de corresponder)
fields.description: Date of death, only if applicable (the specimen's own caption includes '(de corresponder)' — 'if applicable' — an explicit textual optionality signal, unlike most other fields in this document where requiredness is a structurally-inferred judgment call; see VERIFICATION.md). Same ~53pt full-date box width as dateOfBirth.
fields.sourceRef: F.460/F p.1, 1- Datos de Identificación — 'Fecha de fallecimiento (de corresponder)'

fields.name: nationality
fields.label: Argentino/a o Extranjero/a
fields.description: Native PDF radio-button group (field name 'argentino o extranjero', 2 widget instances, exportValues 'argentino'/'extranjero') stating the individual's nationality status.
fields.sourceRef: F.460/F p.1, 1- Datos de Identificación — 'Argentino/a' / 'Extranjero/a' radio

fields.name: countryOfOrigin
fields.label: País de origen
fields.description: Country of origin, chiefly relevant when nationality is 'extranjero' (foreign) — see crossFieldValidation.
fields.sourceRef: F.460/F p.1, 1- Datos de Identificación — 'País de origen'

fields.name: taxResidencyStatus
fields.label: Residente / No residente
fields.description: Native PDF radio-button group (field name 'residenteonoresidente', 2 widget instances, exportValues 'residente'/'no residente') stating the individual's Argentine tax-residency status, independent of nationality (a foreign national can be Argentine tax-resident, and vice versa).
fields.sourceRef: F.460/F p.1, 1- Datos de Identificación — 'Residente' / 'No residente' radio

fields.name: residencyType
fields.label: Tipo de residencia
fields.description: Type of residency permit held, selected from a closed AcroForm combo-box catalog (field name 'tiporesidencia'); chiefly relevant for a foreign resident (see nationality/taxResidencyStatus).
fields.sourceRef: F.460/F p.1, 1- Datos de Identificación — 'Tipo de residencia'

fields.name: residencyValidUntil
fields.label: Vigencia residencia (hasta)
fields.description: Expiration date of the residency permit named in residencyType. ~54pt-wide box, matching this specimen's full-date convention.
fields.sourceRef: F.460/F p.1, 1- Datos de Identificación — 'Vigencia residencia (hasta)'

fields.name: paternalSurname

fields.name: givenNames
fields.label: Nombres (completo)
fields.description: Individual's given name(s) in full. AcroForm field name is 'Apellido.0.1' (a 3-level hierarchy leaf, 'Apellido' → 'Apellido.0' → 'Apellido.0.1') despite the name suggesting a surname field — confirmed as this document's own 'Nombres (completo)' box by its rect (same row as 'Apellido paterno', positioned to its right at x318 under the 'Nombres (completo)' caption at x317/y437), not a surname duplicate.
fields.sourceRef: F.460/F p.1, 2- Datos Referenciales — 'Nombres (completo)'

fields.name: employerType
fields.label: Tipo de empleador/a
fields.description: Native PDF radio-button group (field name 'Tipo empleador', 2 widget instances) stating whether the individual is a regular ('Común') employer or specifically an employer of domestic/household workers ('Personal de casas particulares'). NOTE — a disclosed PDF-authoring quirk found via this document's own independent rect-based verification (not inherited from prior scouting): this group's underlying AcroForm exportValues are literally 'hecho'/'otras' — the same literal strings reused by the unrelated 'Componente de sociedad' radio groups elsewhere in this section — even though the PDF's own adjacent, printed caption text for this specific group reads 'Común' / 'Personal de casas particulares', not 'De hecho' / 'Otras'. This document's `validation.enum` uses the literal exportValues actually submitted by the PDF ('hecho' = 'Común', 'otras' = 'Personal de casas particulares'), per this field's own description, rather than the visually-adjacent-but-differently-worded caption — disclosed rather than silently reconciled, since a consumer filling this PDF programmatically must submit the literal 'hecho'/'otras' values regardless of the visible caption mismatch.
fields.sourceRef: F.460/F p.2, 9- Datos de la Seguridad Social — 'Tipo de empleador/a: Común / Personal de casas particulares' radio

fields.name: employerStartDate
fields.label: Fecha de inicio (como empleador/a)
fields.description: Date the individual began acting as an employer of the type named in employerType. AcroForm box matches this specimen's ~53pt full-date width convention (unlike the narrower ~30pt MM/AAAA boxes used for socialSecurityActivityNStartMonthYear/EndMonthYear); modelled as a full ISO 8601 date rather than a month/year string on that basis — a width-pattern-based judgment call, since the printed caption itself just reads 'Fecha de inicio' with no explicit 'DD/MM/AAAA' or 'MM/AAAA' qualifier (see VERIFICATION.md).
fields.sourceRef: F.460/F p.2, 9- Datos de la Seguridad Social — 'Fecha de inicio'

fields.name: employeeCount
fields.label: Cantidad de personal
fields.description: Number of employees the individual has as an employer of the type named in employerType.
fields.sourceRef: F.460/F p.2, 9- Datos de la Seguridad Social — 'Cantidad de personal'

fields.name: declarationAcknowledged
fields.label: Declaración Jurada (checkbox)
fields.description: Checkbox affirming the sworn declaration ('Declaro que los datos consignados en este formulario son correctos y completos...'). Independent AcroForm checkbox (field name 'Check Box DJ.0', single 'Yes' exportValue). Asserted required on the strength of being unambiguously essential to any valid filing, per this registry's structural-inference practice for a specimen carrying no source-asserted required-field signal (see VERIFICATION.md) — the same basis the personas-jurídicas sibling used for its own declaration-block fields.
fields.sourceRef: F.460/F p.2, 10- Declaración Jurada — checkbox beside 'Declaro que los datos consignados...'

fields.name: declarantName
fields.label: Apellido y nombre (en caso de ser apoderado/a)
fields.description: Full name of the declarant, only where the declarant is signing as an attorney-in-fact ('apoderado/a') rather than the individual themself — the specimen's own caption states the conditionality explicitly ('en caso de ser apoderado/a'). See crossFieldValidation.
fields.sourceRef: F.460/F p.2, 10- Declaración Jurada — 'Apellido y nombre (en caso de ser apoderado/a)'
